professional_summary.comprehensive: I've spent 20+ years at the intersection of geography, demographics, and software — building systems that help people understand who lives where, why it matters, and what to do about it. I found systematic race coding errors in national voter files that nobody else had caught. I built a redistricting platform used by nearly 13,000 analysts. I maintain an open-source geospatial data sciences library with 1,884+ tests. The through-line is always the same: data has a geography, and if you ignore it, you're getting the wrong answer.
professional_summary.data_engineering: Data engineer with 20+ years building infrastructure for demographic and geospatial data at national scale. I built the Civic Graph data warehouse that harmonized polling data from dozens of firms into a dataset representing $1B+ in accumulated investment. I modernized ETL pipelines with PySpark, dbt, and Databricks — cutting processing times by 57% on one engagement. I maintain siege_utilities, an open-source library with 37 GeoDjango models and PySpark/Sedona distributed computing utilities. My geospatial methods return accurate results in roughly 15% of the time that traditional string comparison approaches require.
professional_summary.software_engineering: Software engineer with 20+ years building full-stack systems for data-intensive applications. I architected FLEEM — a Twilio-based polling system handling 10,000+ simultaneous calls — and a redistricting platform with real-time collaborative editing serving 13,000 analysts. I built econometric simulation software for UNICEF and IFRC. I maintain siege_utilities, a production geospatial library with 37 GeoDjango models, PySpark integration, and 1,884+ tests. I care about systems that are correct, not just systems that ship.
professional_summary.gis: I grew up moving across three continents — Queens, Dhaka, Hong Kong, Singapore, New Delhi — and geography has been the lens through which I understand everything since. 20+ years of geospatial work: PostGIS, GDAL, custom tile servers, LiDAR/PointCloud processing, and spatial analysis at every scale from precincts to national boundaries. I maintain siege_utilities, an open-source library with 37 GeoDjango models and Census TIGER integration. I invented a trigonometric boundary estimation algorithm that cut mapping costs 73.5%. Geography isn't a skill on my resume — it's the organizing principle.
professional_summary.product: Product leader with 20+ years shipping data platforms. Managed an 11-person engineering team at Helm/Murmuration, setting technical direction for an enterprise data warehouse serving democratic electoral organizations. Built a redistricting platform used by 12,847 analysts across 89 organizations. At GSD&M, rebuilt a data department from scratch and rewrote their services offering. I understand both the technical architecture and the business case — because I've usually had to build and sell both.
professional_summary.marketing: Marketing analytics professional with 20+ years turning demographic and behavioral data into audience strategy. At GSD&M, I restructured the Decision Sciences Department — introduced spatial analysis, version control, and Agile to a team that had been doing everything in Excel. Built consumer segmentation systems that revealed patterns in existing customer data nobody had seen. Delivered $4.9M in additional revenue through testing and optimization. I bring statistical rigor to creative problems.
professional_summary.data_analysis_visualization: Data analyst with 20+ years and a focus on finding errors everyone else missed. I discovered systematic race coding errors in national voter databases — decades of miscoding that affected every Black and Asian-American voter in the system. Built geospatial ML algorithms that improved classification accuracy from 23% to 64%. I introduced choropleths and hexagonal grid maps to firms that had never seen spatial visualization. My analysis has been cited in Supreme Court proceedings. The job isn't just finding patterns — it's making sure the patterns are real.
professional_summary.polling_research_redistricting: Political researcher and redistricting specialist with 20+ years in survey methodology, electoral prediction, and demographic modeling. I built FLEEM, a polling system that saved $500K+ annually and supported campaigns for Senators Heinrich and Warren. I redesigned sampling methodologies to improve sample-to-universe correspondence, producing more representative surveys and more reliable electoral predictions. I've briefed Presidents, Senators, and Governors on election integrity. My analysis has been used in Supreme Court cases. The instruments I build don't just collect data — they're designed to get the truth.
professional_summary.technical_focused: Senior data scientist and software engineer specializing in geospatial machine learning and large-scale demographic analysis. 20+ years building algorithms, platforms, and infrastructure. Developed demographic classification algorithms improving accuracy from 23% to 64% across 178,000+ precincts. Maintain siege_utilities, a production geospatial library with PySpark/Sedona distributed computing, 37 GeoDjango models, and 1,884+ tests. Built platforms serving 13,000 analysts at national scale.
professional_summary.business_focused: Data scientist with 20+ years delivering measurable outcomes. Saved organizations $4.7M through a single algorithmic innovation. Generated $4.9M in additional revenue through optimization and testing. Cut polling costs by $500K+ annually by building IVR infrastructure in-house. Redesigned sampling methods to produce more representative surveys. Built platforms used by nearly 13,000 analysts. I don't just build models — I build systems that change how organizations make decisions.

key_achievements.demographic_breakthrough: Discovered systematic race coding errors in national voter databases affecting all Black and Asian-American voters — decades of miscoding nobody else had caught. Built geospatial ML algorithms improving classification accuracy from 23% to 64%.
key_achievements.trigonometric_algorithm: Invented a trigonometric boundary estimation algorithm that reduced mapping costs by 73.5%, saving campaigns and nonprofits $4.7M and making redistricting analysis accessible to organizations that couldn't previously afford it.
key_achievements.fraud_detection_systems: Built real-time FEC fraud detection and analysis system (live at elect.info) using Python, Pandas, and PySpark — processing billions of records to identify likely fraud, money laundering, and financial crimes in a political spending sub-economy valued over $2 trillion.
key_achievements.predictive_excellence: Redesigned sampling methodologies to achieve stronger sample-to-universe correspondence, producing more representative surveys and more reliable electoral predictions across presidential, gubernatorial, congressional, and senatorial campaigns.
key_achievements.platform_impact: Built a redistricting platform serving 12,847 analysts across 89 organizations during the 2021 redistricting cycle, with real-time collaborative editing and Census integration.
key_achievements.expert_testimony: Briefed Presidents, Congressmen, Senators, and Governors on election integrity, voter sentiment, and postmortem analysis. Provided expert testimony on electoral data integrity.
key_achievements.regulatory_innovation: Built FEC compliance and demystification platform (elect.info) — turning opaque campaign finance data into transparent, searchable, analyzable records in real time.
key_achievements.methodological_advancement: Developed longitudinal geospatial analysis methods that improved segmentation accuracy by 34% and survey incidence rates by 28%, reducing polling costs while increasing response quality.
key_achievements.revenue_generation: Delivered $4.9M in additional revenue through continuous testing and optimization, with 23% improvement in conversion rates.
key_achievements.data_infrastructure: Built cloud-based data warehouse on AWS processing billions of records with 99.94% accuracy across 178,000+ precincts. Geospatial query methods returned results in ~15% of the time traditional string comparison required.
key_achievements.technical_architecture: Designed ETL pipelines using PySpark, dbt, Databricks, and PostgreSQL/PostGIS — processing large-scale geospatial datasets with automated quality monitoring.
key_achievements.supreme_court_impact: Data analysis cited in Supreme Court case proceedings, with expert methodology validated at the highest judicial level.

siege_utilities.name: siege_utilities — Geospatial Data Sciences Library
siege_utilities.description: Open-source Python library powering all Siege Analytics workflows. 37 GeoDjango models for Census TIGER, NCES, NLRB, and Federal Judicial Districts. 9 population services for demographic analysis. PySpark/Apache Sedona distributed computing utilities. Census API integration for ACS, Decennial, and PL 94-171 redistricting data. Hydra + Pydantic configuration system. 1,884+ tests. Dual-licensed AGPLv3/Commercial.
siege_utilities.impact: Foundation infrastructure for all Siege Analytics projects — every analysis pipeline and data product depends on it. Demonstrates commitment to open-source and rigorous engineering practices.
technical_details: 37 GeoDjango models spanning Census TIGER, NCES school districts, NLRB regions, Federal Judicial Districts
9 population services: demographic, crosswalk, timeseries, urbanicity classification, and more
37+ PySpark/Apache Sedona distributed computing functions for geospatial processing at scale
54+ Hydra + Pydantic configuration and utility functions
Census API integration: ACS, Decennial, PL 94-171 redistricting data
Google Workspace API integration, Snowflake connector
Tiered geospatial installation: geo-lite, geo, geodjango
1,884+ test cases with comprehensive coverage
siege_utilities.technical_description: Production Python library with modular architecture: GeoDjango ORM models, PySpark/Sedona distributed processing, Pydantic validation, and Hydra configuration. Tiered installation system for different deployment contexts.
siege_utilities.business_description: Open-source library that reduces project setup time from weeks to hours by providing pre-built geospatial data models, Census integration, and distributed computing utilities. Dual-licensed for commercial use.
siege_utilities.spatial_description: 37 GeoDjango models covering US Census TIGER geographies at every scale — from blocks to states, plus specialized boundaries (school districts, judicial districts, NLRB regions). 9 population services for demographic analysis with Census API integration for ACS, Decennial, and PL 94-171 redistricting data.

redistricting_platform.name: National Redistricting Platform
redistricting_platform.description: Cloud-based GeoDjango platform for redistricting analysis with real-time collaborative editing and Census integration, used by 12,847 analysts across 89 organizations during the 2021 redistricting cycle.
redistricting_platform.impact: Reduced mapping costs by 73.5%, saving organizations $4.7M. Made redistricting analysis accessible to organizations that previously couldn't afford it.
technical_details: Processed data for all 435 congressional districts and 7,383 state legislative districts
Real-time collaborative editing supporting 156 concurrent projects
Geospatial query methods returned results in ~15% of the time traditional string-based approaches required
Automated compliance checking preventing $1.27M in potential violations
WebSocket-based real-time collaboration with conflict resolution
redistricting_platform.technical_description: GeoDjango/PostGIS application with React frontend, WebSocket real-time collaboration, Docker deployment on AWS. Geospatial queries ~85% faster than string-based approaches, with automated compliance validation.
redistricting_platform.business_description: Platform used by 12,847 analysts across 89 organizations to ensure fair representation. Cut mapping costs 73.5% and saved $4.7M, making redistricting accessible to smaller nonprofits.
redistricting_platform.spatial_description: PostGIS-backed spatial analysis covering all US Census geographies. Trigonometric boundary estimation algorithm. Spatial queries optimized for real-time interactive redistricting across 178,000+ precincts.

fleem_polling_system.name: FLEEM Political Polling System
fleem_polling_system.description: Web application using Twilio API for regulated political surveys, emulating a predictive dialer to handle 10,000+ simultaneous calls. Built for PCCC, used in campaigns for Senators Heinrich and Warren.
fleem_polling_system.impact: Saved PAC $500K+ annually by doing IVR polling in-house instead of contracting with Public Policy Polling. Supported campaigns for Senators Heinrich and Warren.
technical_details: Handled peak loads of 10,000+ simultaneous phone calls via Twilio API
IVR system for automated survey collection with real-time response aggregation
Geospatial reporting system with PostGIS integration
Automated survey deployment and response collection at scale
fleem_polling_system.technical_description: Django/Twilio application emulating predictive dialer functionality — concurrent call management, IVR survey flow, real-time response aggregation, PostGIS-backed geospatial reporting.
fleem_polling_system.business_description: In-house IVR polling system that saved $500K+/year by replacing contracted polling firms. Enabled rapid-cycle research for major campaigns, including early support for Senators Heinrich and Warren.
fleem_polling_system.spatial_description: GeoDjango/PostGIS reporting system mapping survey responses to electoral geographies — precincts, districts, states — for spatially-aware campaign decision-making.

demographic_ml_system.name: Geospatial Demographic Classification System
demographic_ml_system.description: Machine learning system that discovered systematic race coding errors in national voter databases and improved demographic classification accuracy from 23% to 64%. The finding affected all Black and Asian-American voters in the system.
demographic_ml_system.impact: Corrected demographic data affecting all Black and Asian-American voters. Improved electoral prediction accuracy by 22%. Analysis cited in Supreme Court proceedings.
demographic_ml_system.impact_neutral: Corrected demographic data affecting 50M+ voters nationwide. Improved electoral prediction accuracy by 22%. Analysis cited in Supreme Court proceedings.
technical_details: Processed demographic data across 178,000+ precincts and 23,000+ electoral districts
Meta-analysis frameworks for systematic error detection at population scale
Geospatial feature engineering pipelines for demographic classification
178% improvement in classification accuracy (23% to 64%)
demographic_ml_system.technical_description: Scikit-learn/TensorFlow ML pipeline with PostGIS spatial features. Meta-analysis framework for systematic error detection. PySpark for distributed processing across national-scale voter files.
demographic_ml_system.business_description: Demographic classification system that fundamentally improved the accuracy of voter data used across the political analytics industry. Generated $1.2M in consulting contracts.
demographic_ml_system.spatial_description: Geospatial ML using Census/TIGER geography features for demographic classification. Spatial autocorrelation detection across 178,000+ precincts. PostGIS-backed feature engineering at national scale.

civic_graph.name: Civic Graph Multi-Tenant Data Warehouse
civic_graph.description: Multi-tenant data warehouse harmonizing polling data from dozens of firms with different methodologies and encoding systems into a unified, queryable dataset.
civic_graph.impact: Created a dataset representing $1B+ in accumulated investment that became a foundation for modern electoral analytics, serving dozens of polling and mail firms.
technical_details: Harmonized data from firms with completely different methodologies and encoding systems
Built meta-analysis frameworks for systematic cross-firm data integration
Multi-tenant architecture supporting diverse client needs with data isolation
Standardized data models enabling cross-firm compatibility and comparison
civic_graph.technical_description: PostgreSQL/PostGIS multi-tenant architecture with Python ETL pipelines. Meta-analysis framework for harmonizing heterogeneous polling methodologies into standardized schemas.
civic_graph.business_description: Data warehouse serving dozens of polling and mail firms — unified fragmented datasets into a $1B+ resource that became a foundation for modern electoral analytics.
civic_graph.spatial_description: PostGIS-backed warehouse with geo-referenced polling data. Spatial harmonization across firms using different geographic encoding systems.

tile_server_platform.description: Custom tile server for WMS integration enabling interactive visualization of CRM and Census data for political campaigns.

WMS/WFS service integration for real-time data sharing
Optimized tile rendering for sub-second map loading

tile_server_platform.technical_description: Java/GeoTools tile server with OpenLayers frontend. WMS/WFS services for real-time geospatial data sharing. Optimized rendering pipeline for sub-second tile delivery.
tile_server_platform.business_description: Visualization platform that improved campaign contact rates by 53% — gave field teams spatial intelligence they'd never had before.
tile_server_platform.spatial_description: Custom WMS tile server rendering Census and voter data layers. GeoTools backend with spatial indexing for real-time query against campaign CRM data.

musescore_chord_library.name: MuseScore Chord Library Plugin
musescore_chord_library.description: MuseScore Studio 4.6+ plugin replacing the flat palette system with a searchable, filterable chord voicing library for jazz guitar. 787 voicings across 39 chord qualities with fretboard diagrams that insert directly into scores. First pass accepted into the MuseScore ecosystem without revision or objection.
musescore_chord_library.impact: Accepted into MuseScore's plugin ecosystem on first submission. Identified a product gap — MuseScore had no way to manage large voicing libraries — and filled it with a complete solution in days.
technical_details: 787 voicings across 39 chord qualities, all stored in C and transposed automatically
Engineered clipboard bridge workaround for missing MuseScore setDot() API — Swift pasteboard writer on macOS, Win32 API on Windows, Python cross-platform fallback
12 tuning configurations with chord calculator for generating voicing libraries for any tuning
JSON Schema validation, 49+ unit tests, integration test suite
Mac .pkg installer, Windows batch installer, manual install path
musescore_chord_library.technical_description: QML/JavaScript plugin with Swift and Python clipboard bridge components. Engineered a workaround for a missing MuseScore API (setDot) using platform-native clipboard manipulation. JSON Schema validation, automated testing, cross-platform installers.
musescore_chord_library.business_description: Identified a product gap in MuseScore's plugin ecosystem — no way to manage large chord voicing libraries — and shipped a complete solution that was accepted without revision. Demonstrates rapid prototyping and product instinct.
